chunk_summary: This section addresses age-stratified feeding skills and texture milestones, focusing on screening for ARFID in school-age children and adolescents, and guidelines for complementary feeding in infants aged 6–23 months.
learning_objectives: Understand age-specific feeding skills and texture milestones for infants, children, and adolescents.
Learn to screen for ARFID and assess medical risks in school-age children with restrictive eating patterns.
Review complementary feeding guidelines for infants aged 6–23 months based on WHO and IYCF standards.
Explore iron deficiency anemia management as per IAP standard treatment guidelines.
key_facts: ARFID screening is essential for school-age children and adolescents with restrictive eating patterns.
WHO guidelines recommend complementary feeding for infants aged 6–23 months to ensure adequate nutrition.
Indian Academy of Pediatrics (IAP) position paper (2016) emphasizes age-appropriate feeding practices.
IMNCI chart booklet provides structured guidance for child health and nutrition in India.
Iron deficiency anemia management is outlined in IAP standard treatment guidelines.
reference_quote: Complementary feeding of infants and young children 6–23 months (WHO guideline) — IYCF guidelines: Indian Academy of Pediatrics position paper (2016).
document_key_topics: ARFID screening in restrictive eating patterns
Iron deficiency anemia management in children
